method_atom: An order-by-order perturbative treatment of a nonlinear field theory whose linear dynamics have been deformed by a mass-like term, in which the polynomial self- and cross-interaction coefficients among the theory's decoupled component modes are tuned so that a spurious, unstable higher-derivative degree of freedom cancels at each order. A local, nonlinear change of variables then absorbs the mixing between modes and isolates a special family of derivative self-interactions whose equations of motion stay second-order, keeping the effective description consistent.
object_atom: The gravitational field given a nonzero graviton mass (massive gravity), specifically the self-interactions of its helicity-0 (scalar) mode and its nonlinear mixing with the helicity-2 (tensor) mode in the decoupling limit, and the associated Boulware-Deser ghost of the effective field theory.
brief_reason: What carries across is the coefficient-tuning-to-cancel-a-ghost-mode plus the field-redefinition that exposes second-order derivative self-interactions; what is domain-specific is the massive graviton with its helicity-0/-2 modes and the Boulware-Deser problem.
